questions.question: Which OOP principle is demonstrated by method overloading?
questions.explanation: Method overloading is a form of compile-time (static) polymorphism.

questions.question: What is the output of this code?
questions.explanation: This is runtime polymorphism. The actual object is Dog, so Dog's sound() method is called.

questions.question: Can a Java class extend multiple classes?
options: No, Java does not support multiple inheritance of classes
Yes, if they are in the same package
questions.explanation: Java does not support multiple inheritance of classes. A class can extend only one class but implement multiple interfaces.

questions.question: What is encapsulation in Java?
options: Wrapping data and methods in a single unit with restricted access
questions.explanation: Encapsulation is bundling data (fields) and methods that operate on that data, restricting direct access using access modifiers.

questions.question: Which keyword is used to call the parent class constructor?
questions.explanation: The 'super' keyword is used to call the parent class constructor. It must be the first statement in the child constructor.
